sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Radware buys Pynt to bolster pre-production API security
feed.summary: Radware has acquired API security testing specialist Pynt to enhance its API security product line with pre-production testing capabilities. This acquisition aims to close the gap between shift-left and shift-right strategies, offering a unified platform for API discovery, posture management, business logic protection, runtime defense, and testing. Pynt's technology will continue to be available as a standalone offering while also being integrated into Radware's broader security portfolio.

feed.title: Vale Beats 2025 Production Targets as Iron Ore and Copper Hit Highest Levels Since 2018
feed.summary: Vale SA (VALE) reported strong operational results for 2025, exceeding its production guidance across all segments, with iron ore and copper output reaching their highest levels since 2018. The company's fourth-quarter performance showed increased volumes in iron ore, copper, and nickel, along with a strategic shift in its product mix to optimize value amidst weaker pellet premiums. Spark, TipRanks' AI Analyst, rates VALE as Outperform, noting its attractive valuation, strong earnings, and solid financials, despite some concerns about modest revenue growth and technical overbought conditions.

feed.title: Prologis Announces Tax Treatment of 2025 Dividends
feed.summary: Prologis, Inc. has announced the tax treatment for its 2025 distributions for both common and preferred stock, providing detailed exhibits as prescribed by the Internal Revenue Code. Shareholders will receive IRS Form 1099-DIV via Computershare or their brokerage firm. The company advises consulting a tax advisor as federal, state, and local tax laws vary and affect taxpayers differently.

feed.title: Arbor Realty's Huge 16% Yield: Don't Fold Yet (NYSE:ABR)
feed.summary: Arbor Realty Trust (ABR) offers a high 16% dividend yield, but it carries a significant risk of further dividend cuts due to past reliance on one-time gains and investor concerns over credit quality. Despite trading at a low 0.64x book value, ABR could see some relief with falling interest rates in 2026, which may benefit its loan portfolio. The article suggests that while high-risk, ABR's low valuation, insider buying, and potential for a decent yield even after a cut might appeal to enterprising investors.

feed.title: John Deere Announces Major Expansion with Two New U.S. Facilities Coming
feed.summary: John Deere is expanding its U.S. operations with two new facilities: a distribution center near Hebron, Indiana, and an excavator factory in Kernersville, North Carolina. These investments aim to strengthen the American industry, create hundreds of new jobs, and enhance John Deere's supply chain and manufacturing capabilities. The company plans to invest $20 billion in U.S. manufacturing over the next decade.

feed.title: News | Raymond James expands New York footprint at Park Avenue office tower
feed.summary: Investment bank Raymond James is expanding its New York office presence at 320 Park Avenue by subleasing more than 38,000 square feet from Flagstar. This move consolidates its footprint in the corporate-headquarters-heavy corridor, which is known for a tight vacancy rate and high demand from financial services firms. This expansion follows Raymond James's fifth consecutive year of record annual results and strengthens its position as the largest tenant in the Munich Re-owned tower.

feed.title: CF Industries (CF) Maintains Quarterly Dividend at $0.50/Share
feed.summary: CF Industries Holdings, Inc. (CF) has announced it will maintain its quarterly dividend at $0.50 per share, resulting in a forward yield of 2.18%. The company, a leading producer of nitrogen fertilizers, is also involved in strategic initiatives such as a carbon capture project with Exxon. Despite an Altman Z-Score indicating some financial stress, CF Industries demonstrates robust financial health and a strong market position.

feed.title: Consolidated Edison (ED) Raises Dividend by 4.4%
feed.summary: Consolidated Edison (ED) has announced a 4.4% increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.8875 per share, bringing its forward dividend yield to 3.37%. This move reflects the company's dedication to shareholder value within a regulated utilities sector, despite facing challenges like a declining gross margin and moderate leverage. The stock is currently trading within its historical valuation ranges, with technical indicators suggesting it's approaching overbought territory.

feed.title: Consolidated Edison (ED) Announces Increased Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: Consolidated Edison (ED) has declared an increased quarterly dividend of 88.75 cents per share, payable on March 16 to shareholders as of February 18. This represents an annual increase of 15 cents, continuing the company's commitment to shareholder value. Despite a decline in operating margin over the past five years and its Altman Z-Score indicating potential financial distress, the regulated utility maintains a significant market position and a low volatility typical of its sector.

feed.title: Chevron appoints veteran executive Thomas Horton to board
feed.summary: Chevron has appointed former American Airlines Chairman and CEO Thomas W. Horton as an independent director to its board, where he will also serve on the audit committee. Horton, 64, is a partner at Global Infrastructure Partners and holds board positions at major companies like Walmart and GE Aerospace. This appointment comes as Chevron CEO Mike Wirth is reportedly discussing his own departure from the company with the board of directors.

feed.title: US firm Applied Materials opens 8.06 lakh sq.ft office in City
feed.summary: Applied Materials India, a subsidiary of Applied Materials, Inc., inaugurated an 8.06 lakh square-foot workplace at the International Tech Park Bengaluru (ITPL). This expansion is expected to strengthen Karnataka's position as a leading hub for Electronics System Design & Manufacturing (ESDM) and a chip design cluster in India. The new facility, along with existing capabilities like the India Validation Centre and an AI Centre of Excellence in Chennai, underscores Applied Materials' commitment to enhancing India's semiconductor ecosystem.

feed.title: Insider Sale: Chief Accounting Officer of $SLB Sells 13,268 Shares
feed.summary: Howard Guild, Chief Accounting Officer of SLB (NYSE: SLB), sold 13,268 shares for an estimated $659,419 on January 26, 2026, representing about 37.2% of his holdings. This sale is part of a trend of 7 insider sales and no purchases at SLB over the last six months. The article also details recent hedge fund activity, Congressional trading, and analyst ratings for SLB.

feed.title: Stock Yards Bancorp announces all-stock Field & Main merger
feed.summary: Stock Yards Bancorp (SYBT) announced an all-stock merger agreement to acquire Field & Main Bancorp for approximately $105.7 million. This acquisition, expected to close in Q2 2026, will expand Stock Yards' presence in Western Kentucky and Indiana, creating a $10.4 billion-asset franchise. The deal is projected to be 5.7% accretive to earnings per share with minimal capital dilution, and Field & Main's CEO will join Stock Yards' board.

feed.title: Stock Yards Bancorp to acquire Field & Main in $105.7 million deal
feed.summary: Stock Yards Bancorp announced its acquisition of Field & Main Bancorp in an all-stock deal valued at approximately $105.7 million. The acquisition is expected to close in Q2 2026, subject to regulatory and shareholder approvals, and will expand Stock Yards' presence in Western Kentucky. The combined entity will manage approximately $10.4 billion in assets, and the deal is projected to be 5.7% accretive to Stock Yards' earnings per share.

feed.title: CF Industries declares $0.50 per share dividend
feed.summary: CF Industries Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:CF) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.50 per share, payable on February 27, 2026, to stockholders of record as of February 13, 2026. The company will also release its fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 financial results on February 18, 2026, followed by a conference call on February 19, 2026. This announcement follows an optimistic outlook from analysts and recent positive developments including exceeding Q3 2025 earnings expectations and advancing low-carbon initiatives.

feed.title: Air Products and Chemicals raises quarterly dividend by 1.1% to $1.81/share
feed.summary: Air Products and Chemicals (APD) has announced a 1.1% increase in its quarterly cash dividend, raising it from $1.79 to $1.81 per share. This marks the 42nd consecutive year the company has increased its dividend, demonstrating a long-standing commitment to returning value to shareholders. The dividend is payable on May 13, 2025, to shareholders of record as of April 1, 2025.

feed.title: Con Edison declares quarterly dividend, extends utility record
feed.summary: Consolidated Edison, Inc. announced a quarterly dividend of 88.75 cents per share, an increase of 15 cents annually, payable on March 16, 2026. This marks the company's 52nd consecutive annual dividend increase, the longest streak among S&P 500 utilities. The move reflects Con Edison's commitment to investor returns and reliable service during the clean energy transition, with the company targeting a dividend payout ratio between 55% and 65% of adjusted earnings.

feed.title: Vale's iron ore production rises 3% in 2025, surpassing Rio Tinto's Pilbara
feed.summary: Brazilian miner Vale's iron ore production reached 336.1 million metric tons in 2025, marking a 2.6% increase from 2024 and surpassing Rio Tinto's Pilbara operations for the first time since 2018. Vale's output in the fourth quarter of 2025 was 90.4 million tons, up 6% year-on-year. The company aims to regain its position as the world's largest iron ore producer after losing the title in 2019 following a dam collapse.

feed.title: Air Products and Chemicals Raises Quarterly Dividend to $1.81 a Share From $1.79, Payable May 11 to Holders of Record April 1
feed.summary: Air Products and Chemicals (NYSE: APD) has announced an increase in its quarterly dividend to $1.81 per share, up from the previous $1.79. This dividend is scheduled to be payable on May 11 to shareholders who are on record as of April 1.

feed.title: Woodside Energy posts record 2025 production as project pipeline advances
feed.summary: Woodside Energy Group achieved record oil and gas production in 2025, exceeding guidance, and maintained strong reliability across its key assets. The company also made significant progress on major projects like Scarborough and Trion, and strengthened strategic partnerships. Despite a flat share price over the past year, Woodside is focused on future production targets and project delivery.

feed.title: Stock Yards Bancorp (NASDAQ:SYBT) Releases Earnings Results, Beats Estimates By $0.04 EPS
feed.summary: Stock Yards Bancorp (NASDAQ:SYBT) reported quarterly earnings of $1.24 EPS, exceeding analyst estimates by $0.04. The bank also announced a quarterly dividend of $0.32, translating to an annualized yield of 1.9%. Analysts currently rate the stock as "Hold" with an average target price of $83.67.

feed.title: Cisco Systems (NASDAQ:CSCO) Trading Up 2.2% After Analyst Upgrade
feed.summary: Cisco Systems (NASDAQ:CSCO) saw its shares rise by 2.2% after Evercore ISI upgraded the stock to "outperform" and increased its price target to $100, citing AI-driven networking demand and the new "Cisco 360" partner program. Several other analysts also raised their ratings and price targets, contributing to a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating. Despite the positive analyst sentiment, insider transactions show recent net selling, with CEO Charles Robbins and other insiders disposing of a significant number of shares.

feed.title: Lam Research (NASDAQ:LRCX) Shares Up 7% Following Analyst Upgrade
feed.summary: Lam Research (NASDAQ:LRCX) shares rose 7% after Evercore ISI increased its price target to $241, maintaining an "outperform" rating. The stock traded as high as $239.50, closing at $238.46 on Tuesday. Several other firms also hold "buy" or "strong buy" ratings, with a consensus price target of $202.81.

feed.title: CORRECTING and REPLACING -- BridgeBio Pharma Reports Inducement Grants under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4)
feed.summary: BridgeBio Pharma, Inc. issued a corrected and replaced release regarding equity inducement grants. On January 23, 2026, the company's compensation committee approved grants of restricted stock units for an aggregate of 31,428 shares to 11 new employees. These grants were made under BridgeBio’s Amended and Restated 2019 Inducement Equity Plan, in accordance with Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4), to induce the employees to join the company.

feed.title: WDS: Record production and project progress offset lower prices, supporting strong financials
feed.summary: Woodside Energy Group Ltd (WDS) achieved record production in 2025, surpassing guidance and reaching major project milestones, alongside strong asset reliability. Despite a year-over-year decline in revenue and realised prices, the company maintained robust cash flow and project funding through cost discipline and strategic partnerships. This indicates strong financial management offsetting market pressures.

feed.title: Woodside Energy reports record annual production, tops guidance
feed.summary: Woodside Energy announced a record full-year production of 198.9 million barrels of oil equivalent (MMboe) in 2025, surpassing its guidance despite a dip in fourth-quarter output. The strong performance was attributed to sustained plateau production at its Sangomar field and 100% reliability at the Pluto LNG project. The company's unit production costs also fell within its guidance range.

feed.title: Intuitive Surgical Weighs Cardiac AI Milestone Against Rich Valuation Risk
feed.summary: Intuitive Surgical has received FDA clearance for its da Vinci 5 system for minimally invasive cardiac procedures, which includes AI-powered features. While this expansion into cardiac use cases and deeper AI integration could benefit the company, the stock is currently trading at a rich valuation with a P/E ratio significantly higher than the industry average, posing a risk if adoption or usage growth slows. Investors are now focused on how quickly these new capabilities will be adopted and their impact on procedure volumes and market position.

feed.title: CON EDISON ISSUES REQUEST FOR INFORMATION FOR CLEAN, NON-EMITTING RELIABILITY SOLUTIONS
feed.summary: Con Edison has issued a Request for Information (RFI) to gather ideas for clean, non-emitting solutions to address a projected 125-megawatt reliability gap in New York City by summer 2032, which could grow to 750 megawatts by 2036. The company is seeking input from various entities on strategies like energy efficiency, load shifting, dispatchable resources (e.g., battery storage), and non-dispatchable solutions (e.g., distributed solar). These efforts align with the Climate Leadership and Community Protection Act and aim to ensure long-term grid reliability, with responses due by March 6.

feed.title: Boeing looks to build on momentum at SC Dreamliner plant
feed.summary: Boeing is stabilizing and expanding production at its 787 Dreamliner plant in North Charleston, increasing monthly output from five to eight jets, with plans to reach ten. The company also announced a $1 billion expansion to add a second assembly building and 1,000 jobs. This news follows strong financial performance in the fourth quarter, exceeding Wall Street expectations, and a significant reduction in rework hours for the Dreamliner program.

feed.title: American Financial Group Management to Participate in the BofA Securities 2026 Financial Services Conference
feed.summary: American Financial Group, Inc. (NYSE: AFG) announced that its Co-Chief Executive Officers, Carl H. Lindner III and S. Craig Lindner, and Senior Vice President and CFO, Brian S. Hertzman, will attend the BofA Securities 2026 Financial Services Conference on February 10, 2026. While no formal presentation is scheduled, AFG management will hold investor meetings. Related investor materials will be available on AFG’s website prior to the event.

feed.title: GM's salaried global workforce to see reduction in bonus for 2025
feed.summary: General Motors' salaried employees are expected to receive significantly reduced bonus checks for 2025 due to the company's financial performance falling short of targets. The reduction is attributed to factors like tariffs, which impacted GM's overall financial results for the year. Bonus payouts are tied to both individual and company performance metrics.

feed.title: RTX stock price target raised to $227 from $219 at Vertical Research
feed.summary: Vertical Research Partners increased its price target for RTX Corp. (NYSE:RTX) to $227 from $219, maintaining a Buy rating after the company's strong fourth-quarter results. The firm adjusted its EPS estimates for 2026 and 2027 and raised its free cash flow forecast, citing RTX's defense business and conservative guidance for Pratt & Whitney. This positive outlook is supported by RTX's recent outperformance in earnings and revenue against analyst expectations.

feed.title: Venu Holding stock tumbles after announcing public offering of shares
feed.summary: Venu Holding Corporation's stock dropped 16.9% after the company announced a public offering of approximately $75 million in common stock, with an option for underwriters to purchase an additional $11.25 million. The live music venue developer plans to use the proceeds to fund various development projects and complete a real estate purchase in Colorado. The announcement coincided with the release of preliminary Q4 and full-year 2025 results, showing estimated revenue growth and a significant increase in total assets.

feed.title: Vistra Signs Senior Secured Notes Indenture With Wilmington Trust
feed.summary: Vistra has signed a supplemental indenture and completed a $2.25 billion senior secured notes offering. This offering, consisting of $1.0 billion of 4.700% notes due 2031 and $1.25 billion of 5.350% notes due 2036, aims to fund the Cogentrix Energy acquisition, repay existing debt, and support general corporate purposes. The notes are guaranteed by certain subsidiaries and secured by a first-priority lien on collateral.

feed.title: Australia's Woodside posts 13% fall in fourth-quarter revenue
feed.summary: Woodside Energy (WDS.AX) reported a 13% decrease in its fourth-quarter revenue, primarily due to declining global crude prices and softer demand in Australia. The company's average realised price for oil equivalent fell to $57 per barrel from $63 in the previous year, resulting in total revenue of $3.04 billion for the quarter. Despite the drop, Woodside surpassed Visible Alpha's estimate of $2.84 billion.

feed.title: Brown & Brown: Great Insurance Broker's Discount Is Better Now (NYSE:BRO)
feed.summary: Brown & Brown, Inc. (BRO) remains a Buy due to its consistent profitability, and the recent price drop to $75 offers an attractive entry point. Although short-term organic revenue softness is noted, it's attributed to temporary factors like non-recurring items and CAT rate declines. The company's strong M&A pipeline and expected FCF growth, with 2025 FCF per share estimated at $2.08, underpin its long-term value despite increased debt from acquisitions.

feed.title: Prudential Financial (NYSE:PRU) Major Shareholder Acquires $34,985,598.93 in Stock
feed.summary: A major shareholder in Prudential Financial (NYSE:PRU), Insurance Co Of Ame Prudential, recently acquired 1,406,739 shares for over $34.9 million, increasing its total holdings to 7,709,624 shares. The transaction, disclosed on January 23rd, represents a 22.32% increase in the shareholder's position. Prudential Financial shares traded down 1.4% to $107.42, with a market cap of $37.6 billion and a P/E ratio of 14.76, while analysts maintain a "Hold" rating with an average price target of $120.78.

feed.title: BlackRock Adds Gregg Lemkau as Independent Board Director
feed.summary: BlackRock (BLK) has appointed Gregg R. Lemkau, Co-Chief Executive Officer of BDT & MSD Partners and Chairman of DFO Management, as an independent director to its Board. This move expands the board to 19 members, with 16 being independent, and is intended to leverage Lemkau’s extensive experience in investment banking and capital markets to support BlackRock's future growth and long-term strategy. The company highlighted Lemkau's background from BDT & MSD and Goldman Sachs as a valuable addition to the Board's expertise and diverse perspectives.

feed.title: Biotech BridgeBio hands new recruits 31,428 stock units
feed.summary: BridgeBio Pharma announced it granted 31,428 restricted stock units (RSUs) to 11 new employees on January 23, 2026, as inducement awards. These grants, approved by the compensation committee under the Amended and Restated 2019 Inducement Equity Plan, will vest partially on February 16, 2027, with the remainder vesting quarterly thereafter, contingent on continued employment. The company issued a correcting release to amend previously published inaccurate figures.

feed.title: Texas Instruments (TXN) issues Q1 2026 forecast ahead of analyst expectations
feed.summary: Texas Instruments (TXN) announced its Q1 2026 forecast, which projects revenue between $4.32 billion and $4.68 billion and earnings per share between $1.22 and $1.48. The median revenue forecast of $4.5 billion surpasses Wall Street analysts' expectation of $4.42 billion. Following this announcement, TXN stock increased in after-hours trading as the company continues to benefit from high demand for semiconductors.

feed.title: Makarora and Ares complete $2.1 billion acquisition of Plymouth Industrial
feed.summary: Makarora Management LP and Ares Alternative Credit funds have finalized their $2.1 billion all-cash acquisition of Plymouth Industrial REIT, Inc., with Plymouth shareholders receiving $22.00 per share. Following the deal, Plymouth Industrial REIT has been delisted from public exchanges. The acquisition comes amid a legal challenge from Redimere Advisors LLC regarding the merger.

feed.title: Richard Craig Smith Sells 5,910 Shares of AutoZone (NYSE:AZO) Stock
feed.summary: AutoZone VP Richard Craig Smith sold 5,910 shares of AZO stock on January 23rd at an average price of $3,700, totaling $21.87 million. This sale significantly reduced his ownership in the company by 69.23%, leaving him with 2,627 shares. The stock traded up 0.4% at $3,800.59, with analysts holding a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating and an average price target of $4,285 despite the company missing recent EPS estimates.

feed.title: Laura Whitley Purchases 500 Shares of Texas Capital Bancshares (NASDAQ:TCBI) Stock
feed.summary: Director Laura Whitley recently purchased 500 shares of Texas Capital Bancshares (NASDAQ:TCBI), increasing her total stake to 7,961 shares. This insider buying follows a strong quarterly earnings report where the company beat EPS and revenue estimates. The stock has also seen positive analyst sentiment with several firms raising price targets, and institutional investors collectively own 96.88% of the stock.

feed.title: Insider Buying: Texas Capital Bancshares (NASDAQ:TCBI) Director Buys 2,000 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: Texas Capital Bancshares Director Robert Stallings recently bought 2,000 shares of TCBI stock for $41,820, increasing his stake by 0.72%. This transaction follows the bank's strong quarterly earnings, where it surpassed analyst expectations for both EPS and revenue. Several analysts have raised their price targets for TCBI, contributing to a consensus "Hold" rating with an average target of $101.17.

feed.title: UPS to Eliminate 30,000 Positions and Shut Facilities While Cutting Amazon Dependence
feed.summary: United Parcel Service (UPS) plans to eliminate up to 30,000 operational jobs and close 24 facilities in 2026. This move is part of a strategy to reduce its dependence on Amazon deliveries and achieve $3 billion in cost savings. The company aims for a more profitable model by scaling back Amazon shipments, which previously accounted for 12% of its revenue, and increasing automation.

feed.title: Sky Harbour Group Secures $10 Million Promissory Note Financing
feed.summary: Sky Harbour Group's subsidiary, Sky Harbour LLC, has secured $10 million in unsecured promissory note financing from Yorkville. The note bears a 7.75% interest rate and matures in June 2027, with the company also issuing 40,000 Class A common shares to Yorkville. The proceeds will be used for working capital and general corporate purposes, while an AI analyst rates SKYH as "Neutral" due to weak financials despite improving revenue and growth initiatives.

feed.title: Western Digital (WDC): New Buy Recommendation for This Technology Giant
feed.summary: Mizuho Securities analyst Vijay Rakesh has reiterated a Buy rating on Western Digital (WDC) with a price target of $265.00, citing his strong track record in the Technology sector. Despite this new buy recommendation and another from TipRanks – Google’s Google Computer Hardware, corporate insider sentiment on WDC stock is currently negative, with increased insider selling observed over the past quarter. The company recently reported Q4 revenue of $2.82 billion and a net profit of $1.18 billion.

feed.title: Stock Yards Bancorp Reports Record Fourth Quarter Earnings
feed.summary: Stock Yards Bancorp, Inc. announced record earnings for the fourth quarter and full year of 2025, with net income rising to $36.6 million for Q4 and $140.2 million for the full year. This growth was driven by increases in net interest income, loan and deposit expansion, and strong performance from its Wealth Management & Trust division. The company also strategically expanded its branch network and appointed a new market president, demonstrating its commitment to sustained growth.

feed.title: International Paper Q4 Preview: Expectations May Be Too High Even After Guidance Cut (IP)
feed.summary: International Paper (IP) has refocused on its core packaging business after a merger with DS Smith and the divestiture of Global Cellulose Fibers. Despite sequential revenue and EBITDA margin growth in Q3, the company cut its 2026–2027 guidance due to ongoing macroeconomic pressures and European headwinds. Volume gains in North America are impacting margins, while the EMEA region faces structural challenges, leading to a "Hold" rating on the stock due to muted growth prospects and potentially optimistic consensus and fair valuation at current multiples.

feed.title: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) Stock: Jumps As Company Reaches $309M Returns Policy Settlement
feed.summary: Amazon's stock (AMZN) saw a jump after the company reached a $309.5 million cash settlement in a class-action lawsuit concerning its returns and refund practices. The total relief is valued at over $1 billion and includes significant non-monetary improvements to its systems, alongside an acknowledgement of past "process gaps" without admitting wrongdoing. This settlement adds to Amazon's increasing legal and regulatory costs but was met positively by investors, who reacted to the removal of uncertainty surrounding the lawsuit.
